1	# BlastSimulator2026 — Agent Instructions
     2	
     3	**What it is:** Satirical open-pit mine management game (Theme Hospital meets capitalism). Cartoon 3D visuals, blast physics, union strikes, mafia, lawsuits, 3-level campaign.
     4	
     5	## Orchestrator — Pipeline Routing
     6	
     7	If your task does NOT start with `AGENT_ROLE:`, you are the **ORCHESTRATOR**.
     8	
     9	Your job:
    10	1. Fetch issue context via GitHub tools (always — before writing any code)
    11	2. Detect pipeline type from issue labels or prompt keywords
    12	3. Spawn subagents in order, passing only a minimal role header + task
    13	
    14	### Pipeline selection
    15	
    16	| Pipeline | Trigger keywords / labels | Phases in order |
    17	|---|---|---|
    18	| `implement-feature` | new feature, "implement", "add", backlog task | test-writer → implementer → refactorer → validator |
    19	| `fix-bug` | bug, "fix", "broken", "regression", "error" | implementer → validator |
    20	| `review-pr` | "review", PR feedback, "APPROVED", "LGTM" | reviewer |
    21	| `visual-change` | rendering, UI, canvas, Three.js, visual | test-writer → implementer → refactorer → validator → visual-tester |
    22	| `investigate` | "why", "how", "explain", analysis, no code change | implementer (investigate + report only) |
    23	
    24	### Subagent task format
    25	
    26	Pass **exactly** this to every subagent — nothing more:
    27	
    28	```
    29	AGENT_ROLE: <role-name>
    30	SKILL: <skill-name>   ← optional, include when domain knowledge is needed
    31	
    32	## Task
    33	<issue number, feature description, files involved>
    34	```
    35	
    36	The subagent calls `get_agent_context("<role-name>")` on startup before any other action.
    37	Do NOT inline the full agent instructions yourself — let the subagent load them.
    38	
    39	### Agent Handoff Protocol
    40	
    41	Each subagent must report back:
    42	1. Files created/modified
    43	2. Pass/fail status of its verification step
    44	
    45	The **orchestrator** advances to the next phase based on that report.
    46	
    47	## Subagent Bootstrap Protocol
    48	
    49	If your task starts with `AGENT_ROLE:`, you are a **SUBAGENT**.
    50	
    51	**Before doing anything else:**
    52	1. Extract the role name from `AGENT_ROLE: <name>`
    53	2. Call `get_agent_context("<name>")` immediately
    54	3. Read the returned content in full — those are your role-specific directives
    55	4. If a `SKILL:` line is present, also call `get_skill_context("<skill>")` for domain context
    56	5. Then execute the task according to your role instructions
    57	
    58	## Agent and Skill Tools
    59	
    60	```python
    61	list_agents()              # list available role names
    62	get_agent_context(name)    # load full instructions for a role
    63	list_skills()              # list available skill names
    64	get_skill_context(name)    # load full spec for a domain skill
    65	```
    66	
    67	Available roles: `test-writer`, `implementer`, `refactorer`, `validator`, `reviewer`, `visual-tester`
    68	
    69	## Skills
    70	
    71	Detailed specs for each system live in `.github/skills/`. Load with `get_skill_context(name)` before touching that system:
    72	
    73	| Skill | Use when |
    74	|-------|----------|
